title: Chromatic Material Transfer Lab
description: A split-screen material laboratory where the DispersionTest gem floats beside a ShaderBall2, acting as a prismatic lens that refracts light onto the shader ball. The user manipulates dispersion parameters on the gem and observes how the resulting chromatic properties transfer onto the shader ball's surface material in real time, tuning IOR, roughness, metalness, color channels, and transmission to achieve a target spectral signature displayed on a HUD gauge.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements a Chromatic Material Transfer Lab. Load both GLTFLoader models: place DispersionTest.glb on the left at (-2,0,0) slowly rotating, and ShaderBall2.glb on the right at (2,0,0). Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0,2,8) looking at origin. Add an HDR-style environment using PMREMGenerator with a procedural equirectangular scene (gradient sky). Add a DirectionalLight and AmbientLight. Apply MeshPhysicalMaterial to both models. The DispersionTest gem acts as a prismatic source: create a visible colored light beam (three thin cylinders: red, green, blue slightly offset) connecting the gem to the shader ball, animated with subtle wave motion. Build an HTML/CSS overlay control panel with these interactive steps: (1) a slider for IOR (1.0-2.5, default 1.5) that adjusts the gem's MeshPhysicalMaterial.ior and changes beam spread angle, (2) a slider for Dispersion Strength (0-1) that controls color separation width of the three beams, (3) a slider for Transmission (0-1) applied to the shader ball's material.transmission, (4) a slider for Roughness (0-1) applied to the shader ball's material.roughness, (5) a slider for Metalness (0-1) applied to the shader ball's material.metalness, (6) a color picker that sets the shader ball's material.color tint. Display a target panel showing randomized goal values for all 6 parameters. Add a 'Validate' button that checks if all current values are within 0.05 tolerance of targets (color within 15 RGB units per channel). Show a match percentage score (0-100%) and a success banner with particle burst effect when score exceeds 90%. OrbitControls should be enabled. The beam colors should dynamically shift based on IOR and dispersion values using HSL interpolation. All parameter changes must update materials in real time.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  gemRotationY: number,         // current Y-axis rotation of DispersionTest gem in radians
  gemIOR: number,         // index of refraction of the gem material (1.0-2.5)
  dispersionStrength: number,         // color separation width of the prismatic beams (0-1)
  shaderBallTransmission: number,         // transmission value of shader ball material (0-1)
  shaderBallRoughness: number,         // roughness value of shader ball material (0-1)
  beamWavePhase: number,         // animation phase for beam wave motion in radians
  beamSpreadAngle: number,         // angular spread of RGB beams based on IOR in degrees
  redBeamOffsetY: number,         // Y-offset of red beam cylinder in scene units
  greenBeamOffsetY: number,         // Y-offset of green beam cylinder in scene units
  blueBeamOffsetY: number,         // Y-offset of blue beam cylinder in scene units
  controlsInteracted: boolean,         // whether user has interacted with any slider
  materialsApplied: boolean,         // whether MeshPhysicalMaterial has been applied to both models
}
